action.reasoning: Observation: Robot reaches human (dist=0.81m) but angle stays at -3.12 (human behind robot). Spinning at -20 ang_vel doesn't change the angle - robot is physically stuck or human is moving. has_found_human=False means humanoid_detector never triggered (human never in camera view). Analysis: The robot approaches from behind the human. When close and spinning, it gets stuck. The fix: when close to human but facing wrong way, do a FORWARD CIRCLE (lin_vel + ang_vel) to get around to the front. Also need to approach from the front. Key insight: when dist < 3m and |angle| > 0.5, circle around human by moving forward + turning. Decision: Rewrite predict.py with forward-circle maneuver when close but not facing. Also add: approach target point slightly offset from human to avoid getting stuck behind them.
